question: <QUESTION>
orig_question: Provide the superpowers of the superhero called Deathlok.  superpowers refers to power_name; Deathlok refers to superhero_name = 'Deathlok'
orig_masked: Provide the [C31] of the [T9] called [V1].  [C31] refers to [C31]; [V1] refers to [C28] = '[V1]'; [V1] is a value of the column [C28]
orig_evidence: superpowers refers to power_name; Deathlok refers to superhero_name = 'Deathlok'
orig_gold_sql: SELECT T3.power_name FROM superhero AS T1 INNER JOIN hero_power AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.hero_id INNER JOIN superpower AS T3 ON T2.power_id = T3.id WHERE T1.superhero_name = 'Deathlok'

question: <QUESTION>
orig_question: What is the total number of superheroes that have John as their first name?  have John as their first name refers to full_name LIKE 'John%';
orig_masked: What is the total number of [T9] that have [V1] as their [C20]?  have [V1] as their [C20] refers to [C20] LIKE '[V1]%';; [V1] is a value of the column [C20]
orig_evidence: have John as their first name refers to full_name LIKE 'John%';
orig_gold_sql: SELECT COUNT(id) FROM superhero WHERE full_name LIKE 'John%'

question: <QUESTION>
orig_question: Provide the weight and race of the superhero with superhero ID 40.  weight refers to weight_kg; superhero ID 40 refers to superhero.id = 40;
orig_masked: Provide the [C29] and [C17] of the [T9] with [C24] [V1].  [C29] refers to [C29]; [C24] [V1] refers to [C24] = [V1];; [V1] is a value of the column [C24]
orig_evidence: weight refers to weight_kg; superhero ID 40 refers to superhero.id = 40;
orig_gold_sql: SELECT T1.weight_kg, T2.race FROM superhero AS T1 INNER JOIN race AS T2 ON T1.race_id = T2.id WHERE T1.id = 40

question: <QUESTION>
orig_question: Was the total cholesterol status for the patient id 2927464 on 1995-9-4 at the normal level? total cholesterol normal level refers to N < 250
orig_masked: Was the [C49] status for the [C32] [V2] on [V1] at the [C49]? [C49] [C49] refers to N < [V3]; [V1] is a value of the column [C23]; [V2] is a value of the column [C32]; [V3] is a value of the column [C49]
orig_evidence: total cholesterol normal level refers to N < 250
orig_gold_sql: SELECT CASE WHEN `T-CHO` < 250 THEN 'Normal' ELSE 'Abnormal' END FROM Laboratory WHERE ID = 2927464 AND Date = '1995-09-04'

question: <QUESTION>
orig_question: List down most tallest players' name. tallest refers to rank based on the height in descending order; Most tallest players refers to rank = 1
orig_masked: List down most [C122] [T4]' [C126]. [C122] refers to [C122] based on the [C122] in descending order; Most [C122] [T4] refers to [C122] = 1
orig_evidence: tallest refers to rank based on the height in descending order; Most tallest players refers to rank = 1
orig_gold_sql: SELECT player_name FROM (SELECT player_name, height, DENSE_RANK() OVER (ORDER BY height DESC) as rank FROM Player) WHERE rank = 1

question: <QUESTION>
orig_question: What was the highest score of the home team in the Netherlands Eredivisie league? highest score of the home team refers to MAX(home_team_goal)
orig_masked: What was the highest [C94] of the [C94] in the [V1] [T2]? highest [C94] of the [C94] refers to MAX([C94]); [V1] is a value of the column [C5]
orig_evidence: highest score of the home team refers to MAX(home_team_goal)
orig_gold_sql: SELECT MAX(t2.home_team_goal) FROM League AS t1 INNER JOIN Match AS t2 ON t1.id = t2.league_id WHERE t1.name = 'Netherlands Eredivisie'

question: <QUESTION>
orig_question: Provide the account ID, district and region for loan amount greater than USD300,000.  A2 contains district names and A3 contains region names.
orig_masked: Provide the [C1], [T5] and [C25] for [C34] greater than [V1].  [C24] contains [C24] and [C25] contains [C25].; [V1] is a value of the column [C34]
orig_evidence: A2 contains district names and A3 contains region names.
orig_gold_sql: SELECT T1.account_id, T2.A2, T2.A3 FROM account AS T1 INNER JOIN district AS T2 ON T1.district_id = T2.district_id INNER JOIN loan AS T3 ON T1.account_id = T3.account_id WHERE T3.amount > 300000

question: <QUESTION>
orig_question: In superheroes with weight less than 100, list the full name of the superheroes with brown eyes. weight less than 100 refers to weight_kg < 100
orig_masked: In [T9] with [C29] less than [V2], list the [C20] of the [T9] with [V1] [C19]. [C29] less than [V2] refers to [C29] < [V2]; [V1] is a value of the column [C5]; [V2] is a value of the column [C29]
orig_evidence: weight less than 100 refers to weight_kg < 100
orig_gold_sql: SELECT T1.full_name FROM superhero AS T1 INNER JOIN colour AS T2 ON T1.eye_colour_id = T2.id WHERE T1.weight_kg < 100 AND T2.colour = 'Brown'

question: <QUESTION>
orig_question: What was the age of the youngest patient when they initially arrived at the hospital? age refers to SUBTRACT(YEAR(`First Date`),YEAR(Birthday))
orig_masked: What was the [C59] of the [C59] [T3] when they initially [C62] at the [T3]? [C59] refers to SUBTRACT(YEAR(`[C62]`),YEAR([C59]))
orig_gold_sql: SELECT MIN(STRFTIME('%Y', `First Date`) - STRFTIME('%Y', Birthday)) FROM Patient

question: <QUESTION>
orig_question: How many patients with an Ig G higher than normal? Ig G higher than normal refers to IGG >= 2000; Should consider DISTINCT in the final result;
orig_masked: How many [T3] with an [C34] higher than [C34]? [C34] higher than [C34] refers to [C34] >= [V1]; Should consider DISTINCT in the final result;; [V1] is a value of the column [C34]
orig_evidence: Ig G higher than normal refers to IGG >= 2000; Should consider DISTINCT in the final result;
orig_gold_sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT T1.ID) FROM Patient AS T1 INNER JOIN Laboratory AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.ID INNER JOIN Examination AS T3 ON T3.ID = T2.ID WHERE T2.IGG >= 2000
